EN
ERGO NEXT Insurance · 2026 · Principal Product Designer

A design system built for AI agents, not just designers

After ERGO acquired Next Insurance, the product needed a full rebrand across 7 platforms. The old system was unmaintained; designers detached components to make them work.

As the sole designer, I rebuilt it from scratch and encoded intent into the system: purpose-driven names, semantic tokens, intent metadata, and a 1:1 mapping from every Figma component to its counterpart in code.

An agent reads structure, not visuals. The system became something Claude Code and Cursor could build from without guessing.

CompanyERGO NEXT Insurance
RolePrincipal Product Designer
TeamRotating engineering teams
Year2026
The design system in Figma: components with variant sets and semantic naming, next to the same component in Storybook.

EN
ERGO NEXT Insurance · 2026 · Principal Product Designer

Then the system shipped a product: an AI quote funnel cut from 16 steps to 6

Next's edge is AI underwriting, but the funnel still asked users to type in employees, revenue, property, and equipment. The shortest flow ran 16 steps.

I shifted it from "ask" to "gather and validate": the AI pulls business data from public sources, shows it back, and the user confirms or corrects.

I built the funnel in code with Claude Code from the new design system, no separate Figma file; two developers took the prototype to production. I worked with one product manager.

CompanyERGO NEXT Insurance
RolePrincipal Product Designer
Team1 PM, 2 developers
Year2026
The review and validate screen: AI-prefilled, source-tagged fields the user confirms or corrects.
16 → 6 steps
62% reduction

conversion
in the 3 weeks the gather flow ran live
~26 → ~15 min
shortest completion time
The gather-and-validate flow was paused by the legal team for a privacy review; the other funnel redesigns are live in production.
M
Intuit Mailchimp · 2022 · Principal Product Designer, Growth team

A 15,000-pixel homepage that users stopped reading at 2,000

The homepage had become the place to mention every launch: 30+ features with no hierarchy. User interviews, CSAT surveys, and stakeholders all pointed toward adding more information.

I owned the homepage and the conversion funnel, and made the case that reducing information would convert better.

I de-risked the bet with 50+ A/B tests on lower-traffic landing pages (Optimizely and Amplitude) before touching the homepage. Only 4 features converted.

CompanyIntuit Mailchimp
RolePrincipal Product Designer
TeamGrowth team
Year2022
Before and after: the 2021 homepage next to the 2022 refresh.

homepage length
75% shorter

features kept

M
Intuit Mailchimp · 2022 · Principal Product Designer, Growth team

Fewer, better modules moved the numbers within a month

One message ("increase revenue"), one prominent CTA per module, every path pointing to the pricing page.

Replaced the pastel, illustration-heavy brand with photography and product screenshots to match users who had matured into sophisticated marketers.

The new visual language went on to influence the wider Mailchimp product.

RolePrincipal Product Designer

M
Intuit Mailchimp · 2024 · Lead Designer, Core team

A behavior-change roadmap took new-builder adoption from 26% to about 55%

Four years after launch, only 26% of paid users had adopted the new email builder. Months of new features had not moved adoption or satisfaction.

I mapped the resistance to behavior-change levers adapted from the Fogg model (Ability, Motivation, Prompt, Recovery) and built a phased roadmap sequenced for Black Friday.

A persona-driven narrative secured leadership buy-in and reallocated engineering resources across teams. Template replication shipped at about 91% pixel accuracy after research showed content parity mattered more than an exact match.

CompanyIntuit Mailchimp
RoleLead Designer
TeamCore team
Year2024
The phased roadmap sequenced toward Black Friday.

adoption by Black Friday

62% → 69%

BBVA
BBVA · 2017 · Lead UX Designer

The first AI assistant in banking

BBVA's award-winning app still failed a segment of users: people with low digital literacy avoided it, contacted agents over WhatsApp, and overloaded call centers.

I revived an idea the company had rejected earlier, pitched it, and led the design through launch: managing money by conversation instead of navigating an app.

I researched the two ends of the digital-literacy spectrum, low-literacy users in Mexico and early adopters in Spain, and skipped the satisfied middle.

CompanyBBVA
RoleLead UX Designer
TeamAcross 5 offices
Year2017
The Blue assistant conversation UI on a phone.

BBVA
BBVA · 2017 · Lead UX Designer

Launched in 14 countries, still a top feature of the app

With no precedents or tools for conversational banking UI, we tested with real users every two weeks: bodystorming, Wizard of Oz, keyboard-first after the microphone confused people.

BBVA became the first financial institution to offer services inside WhatsApp and Amazon Alexa.

The recommender-system work seeded BBVA's AI Factory, now a unit of 400+ data scientists and ML engineers. Blue is still live as of 2025.

RoleLead UX Designer
TeamAcross 5 offices

BBVA
BBVA · 2016 · Senior, then Lead UX Designer

One banking app for 18 countries

BBVA grew by acquisition, so the same bank felt like a different product in every country. The brief: one app that ships in 18 countries and still flexes to local features and regulation.

I led the initial architecture exploration, then helped build the design system: a fixed global core with clear extension points for each market.

The ranking and rating jump belong to a multi-year program and many teams. What I claim is the systems work underneath.

RoleSenior, then Lead UX Designer
TeamMany teams, multi-year program

BBVA
BBVA · 2018 · Product Designer

Real-time tracking for syndicated loans

Companies borrowing through loan syndicates had no live view of their financing. Tranches, transactions, covenants, and waivers lived in scattered documents, chased by phone and email.

I was a product designer on an international team, covering research, UX, and UI. We shaped the structure, interaction model, and interface together.

The tool shipped as the single digital channel for tracking loans where BBVA acts as the agent bank.

RoleProduct Designer
TeamInternational team

BBVA
BBVA · 2017 · Product Designer, discovery and validation

Blockchain trade payments, approved and funded by BBVA's investment committee

B2B payments had not fundamentally changed since ACH in the 1970s. Five industries alone move more than $18 trillion a year.

Research with 17 executives flipped our assumption: businesses wanted control over cashflow above speed. We pivoted from another platform to an add-on that plugs into the ERPs companies already use.

I helped design a milestone model where smart contracts release payment as goods ship, arrive, and pass inspection. A separate team took it into development; it was ultimately not brought to market.

CompanyBBVA
RoleProduct Designer
TeamOne of three, equal say
Year2017
$18T+
moved yearly by five industries
17
executives interviewed globally
Funded
approved by BBVA's investment committee
V
Vegan Moto Club · 2026 · Personal project, designer and builder

A production website shipped by directing AI coding agents

Finding motorcycle gear without animal products meant contacting manufacturers one by one. A community spreadsheet of ~20 products was the best resource that existed.

I designed and shipped a catalog of 500+ vetted products with automation that keeps it current, directing AI coding agents end to end. No dev team, no Figma file.

Next.js with Notion as the CMS, filtered search, and scheduled re-checks so the catalog does not go stale. Anyone can re-run the performance scores at pagespeed.web.dev.

RoleDesigner and builder
TeamNo dev team
